Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Aside Order Granting Defendant Rob Millss Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via CourtCall:
Mark Chernev, Esq., 415-956-8100 appearing for Rob Mills, Defendant
No other appearances.

Hearing is held. The Court questioned if Mr. Sarkar was present. Mr. Chernev stated no.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PETER DINH Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Aside Order Granting Defendant Rob Millss Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Complaint is DENIED. The motion is made on several grounds (some expressly stated and some not).

To the extent the motion is based on extrinsic fraud, the motion is denied. Moving party failed to show any extrinsic fraud, and at most, attempted to show perjury/intrinsic fraud.
To the extent the motion is based on CCP 473(b) discretionary relief, the motion is denied as no surprise, mistake or excusable neglect is shown. Furthermore, no proposed alternative opposition brief and supporting evidence to the original ANTI-SLAPP motion is submitted with this motion.

To the extent the motion is based on CCP 473(b) mandatory relief, the motion fails to cite any authority for the proposition that such relief is available or what exactly was the attorney's fault that led to default/dismissal.

The motion also mentions reconsideration. To the extent this is a motion for reconsideration, it is untimely and there is no reasonable explanation for not locating relevant evidence in possession of the moving party in a timely fashion (or seek an extension for discovery allowable under ANTI-SLAPP statute).

Additionally, on the merits, nothing presented in this motion would change the outcome of the ruling on ANTI-SLAPP. The claims in this action (as alleged) arose out of Mr. Mills asking Mr. Dinh to sign a new lease without knowledge whether the new lease was materially the same as the old lease and Mr. Mills seeking to terminate Mr. Dinh's tenancy based on Mr. Dinh's refusal to sign the new lease. The Court found that Mr. Dinh failed to carry his burden under the second prong of ANTI-SLAPP and show probability of prevailing on the merits, in this case, evidence that Mr. Mills knew that 2008 written lease existed, all terms of that lease, and nevertheless asked Mr. Dinhto sign a new inconsistent lease. Mr. Dinh still presents no such evidence.

Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not Reported. (D501)

Counsel for plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ROB MILLS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS AFTER ORDER GRANTING SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE is GRANTED in the amount of $47.374.00 for attorney's fees reasonably incurred in bringing the successful ANTI-SLAPP motion.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-06-2021 Continued To Oct-27-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Jul-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT ROB MILLS' NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE is GRANTED. Mr. Mills satisfied his first prong burden showing that both of the causes of action alleged by plaintiff Peter Dinh arise out of protected activity. Both causes of action arise from the allegations that Mr. Mills asked Mr. Dinh to sign a new lease without knowledge whether the new lease was materially the same as the old lease and Mr. Mills sought to terminate Mr. Dinh's tenancy based on Mr. Dinh's refusal to sign the new lease. Both of these actions are protected activity by the anti-SLAPP statute. Mr. Dinh failed to satisfy his second prong burden to show that there is minimal merit to either of his causes of action. The admissible evidence shows that the actions taken by Mr. Mills were not done in bad faith and are immunized from liability by the litigation privilege and Mr. Dinh has presented no admissible evidence otherwise. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
